blocks:
سُورة المُؤمن مكّيّة

Ibn Abbas and Qatadah said that all of it is [Makkan] except for two verses from it which were revealed in Medina: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُجادِلُونَ ** up to His saying لا يَعْلَمُونَ. The number of its verses is eighty-five verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) حم Its interpretation has preceded. And in al-Ma’ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): As for حم , its meaning is al-Hamīd (the Praiseworthy), al-Majīd (the Glorious). (٢) تَنْزِيلُ الْكِتابِ مِنَ اللهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْعَلِيمِ. (2 The sending down of the Book is from Allah, the All-mighty, the All-knowing,) (٣) غافِرِ الذَّنْبِ وَقابِلِ التَّوْبِ شَدِيدِ الْعِقابِ ذِي الطَّوْلِ Possessor of grace (ذي الفضل) by abandoning the deserved punishment. لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ** (There is no god except Him), so complete devotion to His worship is obligatory. إِلَيْهِ الْمَصِيرُ ** (To Him is the return), so He will reward the obedient and the disobedient.

(٤) ما يُجادِلُ فِي آياتِ اللهِ بالطعن فيها وادحاض الحقّ ** إِلَّا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (4) No one disputes concerning the signs of Allah except those who disbelieve.

In al-Ikmal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, he said: "The disputers concerning the religion of Allah were cursed upon the tongue of seventy prophets, and whoever disputes ** فِي آياتِ اللهِ ** (concerning the signs of Allah) has disbelieved." Then he recited this Ayah.

And it is narrated from him صلى الله عليه وآله: "That a dispute (جدال) concerning the Quran is disbelief." And it was made indefinite (نكر) because disputation (الجدال) is permissible for solving its complexities (عقده), extracting its realities (حقائقه), cutting off the clinging of the people of deviation (أهل الزّيغ) to it, and refuting their slanders (مطاعنهم) against it. ** فَلا يَغْرُرْكَ تَقَلُّبُهُمْ فِي الْبِلادِ ** (So do not let their movement throughout the lands deceive you) with profitable trades (بالتجارات المربحة). For they will soon be seized for their disbelief, a seizure like that of those before them.

(٥) كَذَّبَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ وَالْأَحْزابُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ ** (5) The people of Noah denied before them, and the confederates after them. And those who formed factions (تحزّبوا) against the Messengers and showed enmity toward them (ناصبوهم) after the people of Noah, such as ʿĀd and Thamūd. وَهَمَّتْ كُلُّ أُمَّةٍ ** from these [nations] بِرَسُولِهِمْ لِيَأْخُذُوهُ so that they might be able to inflict upon him what they desired of tormenting him (تعذيبه). وَجادَلُوا بِالْباطِلِ ** with that which has no reality لِيُدْحِضُوا بِهِ الْحَقَ ** so that they might remove/abolish it thereby. فَأَخَذْتُهُمْ ** with destruction, as a recompense for them. فَكَيْفَ كانَ عِقابِ ** For you pass by their dwellings and see its trace, or you recite their stories in the Quran, and this is an affirmation (تقرير) containing astonishment (تعجيب).

(٦) وَكَذلِكَ حَقَّتْ كَلِمَةُ رَبِّكَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَنَّهُمْ أَصْحابُ النَّارِ (6) And thus the word of your Lord became due against those who were faithless, that they are the inmates of the Fire. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He means Banu Umayyah.

(٧) الَّذِينَ يَحْمِلُونَ الْعَرْشَ وَمَنْ حَوْلَهُ يُسَبِّحُونَ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ ** (7) Those who bear the Throne, and those who are around it, celebrate the praise of their Lord They mention Allah with the comprehensive forms of praise (الثّناء) from the attributes of Majesty (الجلال) and Generosity (الإِكرام). وَيُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ ** and have faith in Him, He informed about their faith (الإِيمان) to manifest His favor and to glorify those who possess it. وَيَسْتَغْفِرُونَ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا and plead for forgiveness for those who have faith: In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): [They plead] for those who have faith in our wilayah (ولايتنا). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah has angels who cause sins to fall from the backs of our followers (شيعتنا), just as the wind causes leaves to fall during their season of falling. And that is His saying, the Almighty: الَّذِينَ يَحْمِلُونَ الْعَرْشَ ** [the rest of] the Ayah. He said: Their seeking forgiveness (استغفارهم), by Allah, is for you, exclusive of this [other] creation. رَبَّنا ** They say: رَبَّنا وَسِعْتَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ رَحْمَةً وَعِلْماً فَاغْفِرْ لِلَّذِينَ تابُوا وَاتَّبَعُوا سَبِيلَكَ وَقِهِمْ عَذابَ الْجَحِيمِ. ‘Our Lord! You embrace all things in mercy and knowledge. So forgive those who repent and follow Your way, and shield them from the punishment of hell.

(٨) رَبَّنا وَأَدْخِلْهُمْ جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ الَّتِي وَعَدْتَهُمْ وَمَنْ صَلَحَ مِنْ آبائِهِمْ وَأَزْواجِهِمْ وَذُرِّيَّاتِهِمْ ** (8) Our Lord! Admit them into the Gardens of Eden which You have promised them, and those who are righteous among their fathers, their spouses, and their descendants. so that their joy may be complete. إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْعَزِيزُ ** the One Whom no decreed thing (مقدور) can resist Him, الْحَكِيمُ the All-wise, the One Who does not act except according to what His wisdom necessitates. And among that is the fulfillment of the promise (الوفاء بالوعد).

(٩) وَقِهِمُ السَّيِّئاتِ وَمَنْ تَقِ السَّيِّئاتِ يَوْمَئِذٍ فَقَدْ رَحِمْتَهُ وَذلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ. (9) And save them from the evils. Whomever You save from the evils on that day, You have certainly had mercy on him, and that is the great success.

Al-Qummi (said) regarding الَّذِينَ يَحْمِلُونَ الْعَرْشَ (those who bear the Throne): means the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Successors (الأوصياء) peace be upon them after him, who bear the knowledge (علم) of Allah. And regarding وَمَنْ حَوْلَهُ (and those around Him): means the angels (الملائكة). And regarding لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا (for those who have faith): means the followers (شيعة) of the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them). And regarding لِلَّذِينَ تابُوا (for those who repent): (it refers to those who repent) from the guardianship (ولاية) of so-and-so and so-and-so and Banu Umayyah, and وَاتَّبَعُوا سَبِيلَكَ (and follow Your way), meaning the guardianship (ولاية) of the Wali (وليّ) of Allah. And regarding وَمَنْ صَلَحَ (and whoever is righteous): means whoever takes Ali (peace be upon him) as his guardian (تولّى), and that is their righteousness (صلاحهم). And regarding فَقَدْ رَحِمْتَهُ (You have certainly had mercy on him): means on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). And وَذلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ (and that is the great success) is for the one whom Allah saves from these, meaning the guardianship (ولاية) of so-and-so and so-and-so.

And in al-Kafi, narrated marfu'an (مرفوعاً): Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, granted the repentant three qualities (خصال). Had He granted one of these qualities to all the inhabitants of the heavens and the earth, they would have been saved by it. Then he recited this Ayah.

(10) ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا يُنادَوْنَ ** on the Day of Resurrection, and it will be said to them: ** لَمَقْتُ اللهِ أَكْبَرُ مِنْ مَقْتِكُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ ** (10) Indeed, those who disbelieved will be called out: 'Allah's abhorrence [of you] is greater than your abhorrence of yourselves,'

meaning: Allah's abhorrence of you is greater than your abhorrence of yourselves, the souls (الأنفس) that command evil (الأمّارة بالسوء), ** إِذْ تُدْعَوْنَ إِلَى الْإِيمانِ فَتَكْفُرُونَ ** when you were invited to the faith, but you disbelieved.

Al-Qummi (said regarding): ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (Indeed, those who disbelieved) means Banu Umayyah. ** إِلَى الْإِيمانِ ** (to the faith) means to the wilāyah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him).

** قالُوا رَبَّنا أَمَتَّنَا اثْنَتَيْنِ وَأَحْيَيْتَنَا اثْنَتَيْنِ. (They will say, 'Our Lord, You have made us die twice, and given us life twice.) Al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That is concerning the Return (الرجعة). I say: Perhaps the intended meaning is that the duality (التّثنية) is only realized through the Return (الرجعة), or they say that during the Return (الرجعة) due to the two instances of bringing to life (الإِحياء) and causing to die (الإماتة) which occur in the grave for the questioning (للسّؤال). فَاعْتَرَفْنا بِذُنُوبِنا فَهَلْ إِلى خُرُوجٍ مِنْ سَبِيلٍ ** (Now we confess our sins. Is there any way out?) Is there any kind of way out from the punishment, by a path which we may take? And they only say that out of the extremity of their despair, seeking an excuse (تعلّلاً) and being bewildered (تحيّراً). And for that reason, they were answered with what they were answered.

(١٢) ذلِكُمْ ** الّذي أنتم فيه ** بِأَنَّهُ ** بسبب أنّه ** إِذا دُعِيَ اللهُ وَحْدَهُ كَفَرْتُمْ ** بالتوحيد ** وَإِنْ يُشْرَكْ بِهِ تُؤْمِنُوا ** بالإِشراك. (12) ذلِكُمْ بِأَنَّهُ إِذا دُعِيَ اللهُ وَحْدَهُ كَفَرْتُمْ وَإِنْ يُشْرَكْ بِهِ تُؤْمِنُوا فَالْحُكْمُ لِلَّهِ الْعَلِيِّ الْكَبِيرِ** (That is because when Allah was invoked alone, you disbelieved, and if partners were ascribed to Him, you believed. So the judgment belongs to Allah, the All-exalted, the All-great.)

** ذلِكُمْ ** (That) which you are in ** بِأَنَّهُ ** (is) because ** إِذا دُعِيَ اللهُ وَحْدَهُ كَفَرْتُمْ ** (when Allah was invoked alone, you disbelieved) in monotheism (بالتوحيد). ** وَإِنْ يُشْرَكْ بِهِ تُؤْمِنُوا ** (and if partners were ascribed to Him, you believed) in polytheism (بالإِشراك).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He says: When Allah alone is mentioned with the guardianship (بولاية) of one whose guardianship Allah has commanded, you disbelieved (كَفَرْتُمْ). And if partners are ascribed to Him (وَإِنْ يُشْرَكْ بِهِ)—meaning one who does not possess guardianship (ولاية)— تُؤْمِنُوا ** (you believe) that he possesses guardianship (ولاية).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): ** إِذا دُعِيَ اللهُ وَحْدَهُ ** (When Allah was invoked alone)—and the People of Guardianship (أهل الولاية)—you disbelieved (كَفَرْتُمْ). ** فَالْحُكْمُ لِلَّهِ الْعَلِيِّ الْكَبِيرِ ** (The judgment belongs to Allah, the All-exalted, the All-great), [Who is far] from having partners ascribed to Him or being equated with others, since He has judged you with eternal punishment (بالعذاب السرمد).

(١٣) هُوَ الَّذِي يُرِيكُمْ آياتِهِ ** (13) It is He who shows you His signs (meaning) those indicating al-Tawhid (التوحيد) and the rest of what must be known, وَيُنَزِّلُ لَكُمْ مِنَ السَّماءِ رِزْقاً (and sends down for you provision from the sky) (meaning) the means (أسباب) of provision (رزق). وَما يَتَذَكَّرُ إِلَّا مَنْ يُنِيبُ ** (But none takes admonition except him who turns penitently [to Him]) (meaning) he turns away from denial (الإنكار) by turning towards them (the signs) and reflecting upon them.

(14) ** فَادْعُوا اللهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ ** (So supplicate Allah, dedicating your worship to Him) [meaning] from polytheism (الشرك), وَلَوْ كَرِهَ الْكافِرُونَ ** (though the faithless should be averse.) [meaning] to your sincerity (إخلاصكم) and it is difficult for them.

(15) ** رَفِيعُ الدَّرَجاتِ ذُو الْعَرْشِ يُلْقِي الرُّوحَ مِنْ أَمْرِهِ عَلى مَنْ يَشاءُ مِنْ عِبادِهِ. (Exalter of ranks, Possessor of the Throne, He casts the Spirit of His command upon whomever of His servants He wishes,) القمّيّ said: It is the Holy Spirit (روح القدس), and it is specific to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Imams (الأئمّة) عليهم السلام, لِيُنْذِرَ يَوْمَ التَّلاقِ ** (that He may warn of the Day of Encounter.) [meaning] the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة).

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, and القمّيّ said: It is the Day when the inhabitants of the heaven and the inhabitants of the earth meet.

(١٦) يَوْمَ هُمْ بارِزُونَ ** (The day when they will emerge) (They will be) emerging from their graves; nothing will conceal them. لا يَخْفى عَلَى اللهِ مِنْهُمْ شَيْءٌ ** (nothing about them will be hidden from Allah.) (This refers to) their persons (أعيانهم), their deeds (أعمالهم), and their states (أحوالهم). لِمَنِ الْمُلْكُ الْيَوْمَ لِلَّهِ الْواحِدِ الْقَهَّارِ (To whom does the sovereignty belong today? To Allah, the One, the All-dominant!) (This is) a narration of what He will ask about and what He will answer with, based on what the apparent state of affairs indicates therein, concerning the vanishing of means (زوال الأسباب) and the removal of intermediaries (ارتفاع الوسائط). As for the reality of the situation (حقيقة الحال), it speaks of this always (دائماً).

** الْيَوْمَ تُجْزى كُلُّ نَفْسٍ بِما كَسَبَتْ لا ظُلْمَ الْيَوْمَ إِنَّ اللهَ سَرِيعُ الْحِسابِ ** (17) This day every soul shall be recompensed for what it has earned. There will be no injustice this day. Indeed, Allah is swift at reckoning. Since no affair distracts Him from another affair.

In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in the tradition explaining the letters, He said: And the letter Mīm (الميم) signifies the dominion (مُلْك) of Allah on the Day when there is no sovereign other than Him. And Allah says: لِمَنِ الْمُلْكُ الْيَوْمَ ** (To whom belongs the sovereignty today?) Then the souls of His Prophets, His Messengers, and His Proofs (حججه) speak, and they say: لِلَّهِ الْواحِدِ الْقَهَّارِ ** (To Allah, the One, the All-subduing.) Then Allah, majestic is His glory (جلّ جلاله), says: **الْيَوْمَ تُجْزى ** (This day every soul shall be recompensed...) [up to] the end of the Ayah.

And in Nahj al-Balagha: And indeed, He, glory be to Him (سبحانه), returns after the annihilation (فناء) of the world, alone, with nothing alongside Him, just as He was before its beginning. Likewise, He will be after its annihilation, without time (وقت), or place (مكان), or moment (حين), or era (زمان). At that point, the appointed terms (الآجال) and times (الأوقات) cease to exist, and the years (السّنون) and hours (الساعات) vanish. There is nothing except the One, the All-subduing (الواحد القهّار), to Whom is the return (مصير) of all affairs. The beginning of their creation was without any power (قدرة) on their part, and their annihilation was without any resistance (امتناع) on their part. Had they been able to resist (الامتناع), their existence (بقاؤها) would have endured. Another tradition concerning this meaning has already passed at the end of Surat al-Zumar.

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in the tradition concerning Allah causing the death (إماتة) of the inhabitants of the earth, the inhabitants of the heaven, and the angels. He said: Then He remains for a period equal to the time He created the creation, and the like of all that, and multiples of that. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), says: لِمَنِ الْمُلْكُ الْيَوْمَ ** (To whom belongs the sovereignty today?) Then He replies to Himself: لِلَّهِ الْواحِدِ الْقَهَّارِ ** (To Allah, the One, the All-subduing.) "Where are the tyrants (الجبّارون)? Where are those who claimed another god alongside Me? Where are the arrogant (المتكبّرون) and their pride (نخوتهم)?" Then He resurrects the creation.

(١٨) وَأَنْذِرْهُمْ يَوْمَ الْآزِفَةِ ** (18) And warn them of the Day of the Imminent [Doom] meaning the Resurrection (القيامة). It was named thus because of its imminence (لُازوفها), meaning its nearness (قربها). إِذِ الْقُلُوبُ لَدَى الْحَناجِرِ when the hearts are lodged in the throats for they rise up from their places and cling to their throats, so they neither return [to their places] so that they may breathe (فيتروّحوا), nor do they exit so that they may rest (فيستريحوا), كاظِمِينَ ** suppressing [their grief]. due to grief (الغمّ). Al-Qummi said: [They are] grieved (مغمومين), distressed (مكروبين). ما لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِنْ حَمِيمٍ ** The wrongdoers will have no sympathizer a close, compassionate relative (قريب مشفق), وَلا شَفِيعٍ يُطاعُ nor any intercessor who will be obeyed. who intercedes (يشفع).

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): There is no believer who commits a sin except that it grieves him and he regrets it. And the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: Regret (النّدم) suffices as repentance (توبة). And He said: Whoever is pleased by his good deed and grieved by his evil deed, he is a believer (مؤمن). For whoever does not regret a sin he commits is not a believer (مؤمن), and intercession (الشفاعة) will not be obligatory for him, and he will be a wrongdoer (ظالماً). And Allah the Almighty says: ما لِلظَّالِمِينَ مِنْ حَمِيمٍ وَلا شَفِيعٍ يُطاعُ. (The wrongdoers will have no sympathizer nor any intercessor who will be obeyed.)

(١٩) يَعْلَمُ خائِنَةَ الْأَعْيُنِ ** (19) He knows the treachery of the eyes Stealing a glance (استراق النّظر). In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about its meaning, and He said: "Have you not seen a man looking at a thing as if he is not looking at it? That is خائِنَةَ الْأَعْيُنِ. (the treachery of the eyes)." And in al-Majma', in the narration concerning Ibn Abi Sarh: 'Abbad ibn Bishr said to him: "O Messenger of Allah, my eye has remained fixed on your eye, waiting for you to signal to me so that I may kill him." He (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "Indeed, the Prophets do not commit the treachery of the eyes (خائِنَةَ الْأَعْيُنِ)." وَما تُخْفِي الصُّدُورُ ** and what the breasts conceal. meaning the inner thoughts (الضمائر).

(٢٠) وَاللهُ يَقْضِي بِالْحَقِّ وَالَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ ** (20) And Allah judges with justice, while those whom they invoke besides Him And it was read with the tāʾ (ت) لا يَقْضُونَ بِشَيْءٍ ** do not judge with anything. (This is) mockery of them. إِنَّ اللهَ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْبَصِيرُ ** Indeed, Allah is the All-hearing, the All-seeing. (This is) confirmation of His knowledge concerning the treachery of the eyes (خائنة الأعين), and His judgment with justice (بالحقّ), and a threat to them regarding what they say and what they do, and an allusion to the state of that which يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِهِ.** (they invoke besides Him.)

(٢١) أَوَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَيَنْظُرُوا كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ كانُوا مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (Have they not travelled in the land so that they may observe how was the fate of those who were before them?) [Observe] the state of those who denied the messengers before them, such as 'Ād and Thamūd. كانُوا هُمْ أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً (They were mightier than them in strength), meaning: power (قدرة) and dominance (تمكّنا). And it was read (وقرئ) as مِنْكُمْ (than you). وَآثاراً فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (and in monuments in the land) such as fortresses (القلاع) and fortified cities (المدائن الحصينة). فَأَخَذَهُمُ ** (So He seized them) due to their sins (بذنوبهم). وَما كانَ لَهُمْ مِنَ اللهِ مِنْ واقٍ ** (and they had no one to protect them from Allah) who could avert the punishment (العذاب) from them.

(٢٢) ذلِكَ ** The seizing/punishment is because they: كانَتْ تَأْتِيهِمْ رُسُلُهُمْ بِالْبَيِّناتِ فَكَفَرُوا فَأَخَذَهُمُ اللهُ (Their apostles used to bring them manifest proofs, but they disbelieved, so Allah seized them.) إِنَّهُ قَوِيٌ He is fully capable of executing what He wills, with the ultimate degree of capability (التمكّن), شَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ** Severe in punishment; no attention is paid to a punishment lesser than His punishment.

(٢٣) وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا مُوسى بِآياتِنا ** with the miracles (بالمعجزات) ** وَسُلْطانٍ مُبِينٍ ** and an overwhelming, clear proof (وحجّة قاهرة ظاهرة). (23) Certainly We sent Moses with Our signs

(٢٤)** إِلى فِرْعَوْنَ وَهامانَ وَقارُونَ فَقالُوا ساحِرٌ كَذَّابٌ ** They meant Moses (peace be upon him). (24) to Pharaoh and Haman and Korah. But they said, 'A lying sorcerer.'

(٢٥) فَلَمَّا جاءَهُمْ بِالْحَقِّ مِنْ عِنْدِنا قالُوا اقْتُلُوا أَبْناءَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ وَاسْتَحْيُوا نِساءَهُمْ ** meaning: Restore upon them what you used to do to them previously, so that they might be deterred from supporting Moses. ** وَما كَيْدُ الْكافِرِينَ إِلَّا فِي ضَلالٍ ** in loss (في ضياع). (25) So when he brought them the truth from Us, they said, 'Kill the sons of those who have believed with him, and spare their women.' But the stratagem of the faithless is only in futility

** وَقالَ فِرْعَوْنُ ذَرُونِي أَقْتُلْ مُوسى وَلْيَدْعُ رَبَّهُ ** (26) 'Pharaoh said, "Let me kill Moses, and let him call his Lord!"' He said this out of boldness (تجلّداً) and lack of concern (عدم مبالاة) for his prayer/supplication (بدعائه). It is said that they used to restrain him from killing him, saying: "He is not the one you fear, rather he is a sorcerer (ساحر). If you kill him, it will be thought that you were unable to counter him with proof (بالحجّة)." And his making excuses (تعلّله) with that, despite his being a shedder of blood (سفّاكاً) in the easiest matter (في أهون شيء), is proof (دليل) that he was certain (تيقّن) that he was a Prophet, so he feared killing him, or he thought that if he attempted it (حاوله), it would not be made easy for him (لم يتيسّر له).

In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, "What prevented him?" He said: "His guidance (رشدته) prevented him. Prophets and the children of Prophets are not killed except by the children of fornication (أولاد الزنا)."

**إِنِّي أَخافُ ** 'Indeed, I fear' (if I do not kill him)

أَنْ يُبَدِّلَ دِينَكُمْ ** 'that he will change your religion' That he will change what you are upon, regarding His worship (عبادته) and the worship of idols (عبادة الأصنام), like His saying: وَيَذَرَكَ وَآلِهَتَ (and leave you and your gods).

أَوْ أَنْ يُظْهِرَ فِي الْأَرْضِ الْفَسادَ 'or bring about corruption in the land.' That which corrupts your worldly life (دنياكم) through warfare (التحارب) and mutual killing (التهارج). And it was read (وقرئ) with the waw (بالواو) in the sense of conjunction (على معنى الجمع), and with the opening (بفتح) of the ya (الياء) and the ha (الهاء), and the nominative case (رفع) of al-fasād (الفساد).

(٢٧) ** وَقالَ مُوسى ** (27) And Moses said, أي لقومه لمّا سمع كلامه إِنِّي عُذْتُ بِرَبِّي وَرَبِّكُمْ مِنْ كُلِّ مُتَكَبِّرٍ لا يُؤْمِنُ بِيَوْمِ الْحِسابِ. meaning: to his people when he heard his (Pharaoh's) speech: (27) 'Indeed I seek refuge in my Lord and your Lord from every arrogant one who does not believe in the Day of Reckoning.'

(٢٨) ** وَقالَ رَجُلٌ مُؤْمِنٌ مِنْ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ ** (28) And a faithful man from Pharaoh's clan said, من أقربائه. meaning: from his relatives (أقربائه). في العيون عن الرضا عليه السلام : كان ابن خاله وفي خبر آخر : كان ابن عمّه كما يأتي يَكْتُمُ إِيمانَهُ ** In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was his maternal uncle's son (ابن خاله). And in another narration (خبر آخر): He was his paternal uncle's son (ابن عمّه), as will follow. who concealed his faith, القمّيّ قال كتم إيمانه ستّ مائة سنة. Al-Qummi said: He concealed his faith for six hundred years. وفي المجمع عن الصادق عليه السلام : التقيّة من ديني ودين آبائي ولا دين لمن لا تقيّة له والتقيّة ترس الله في الأرض لأنّ مؤمن آل فرعون لو أظهر الإِسلام لقتل. In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Al-Taqiyyah (التقيّة) is part of my religion and the religion of my forefathers, and there is no religion for one who has no Taqiyyah (تقيّة). And al-Taqiyyah (التقيّة) is Allah's shield on earth, because if the believer from Pharaoh's clan had revealed his Islam, he would have been killed. وفي المجالس عن النبيّ صلى الله عليه وآله : الصدّيقون ثلاثة وعدّ منهم حزقيل مؤمن آل فرعون وقد مرّ تمامه أَتَقْتُلُونَ رَجُلاً ** In al-Majalis, narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): The truthful ones (الصدّيقون) are three, and he counted among them Hizqil (حزقيل), the believer from Pharaoh's clan. Its entirety has already passed. 'Are you going to kill a man أتقصدون قتله أَنْ يَقُولَ ** لأن يقول رَبِّيَ اللهُ ** وحده Do you intend to kill him because he says, because he says, 'My Lord is Allah' alone? وَقَدْ جاءَكُمْ بِالْبَيِّناتِ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ ** while he has brought you manifest proofs from your Lord? أضافه إليهم بعد ذكر البيّنات احتجاجاً عليهم واستدراجاً لهم إلى الاعتراف به ثمّ أخذهم بالاحتجاج من باب الاحتياط وَإِنْ يَكُ كاذِباً فَعَلَيْهِ كَذِبُهُ لا يتخطّاه وبال كذبه فيحتاج في دفعه الى قتله He attributed Him (Allah) to them after mentioning the manifest proofs, as an argument against them and to gradually lead them to acknowledge Him. Then he took them to task with the argument from the perspective of caution (الاحتياط): If he is a liar, his lie will be upon him (فَعَلَيْهِ كَذِبُهُ). The evil consequence (وبال) of his lie will not pass beyond him, so there is no need to kill him to ward it off. وَإِنْ يَكُ صادِقاً يُصِبْكُمْ بَعْضُ الَّذِي يَعِدُكُمْ ** But if he is truthful, some of what he promises you will befall you.' فلا أقلّ من أن يصيبكم بعضه وفيه مبالغة في التحذير وإظهار للانصاف وعدم التعصّب ولذلك قدّم كونه كاذباً إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَهْدِي مَنْ هُوَ مُسْرِفٌ كَذَّابٌ ** The least that can happen is that some of it will befall you. In this is an exaggeration in warning, and a demonstration of fairness (الإنصاف) and lack of fanaticism (عدم التعصّب). For this reason, he presented the case of him being a liar first. Indeed Allah does not guide him who is a transgressor, a liar. قيل احتجاج ثالث ذو وجهين أحدهما انّه لو كان مسرفاً كذّاباً لما هداه الله الى البيّنات ولما عضده بتلك المعجزات وثانيهما انّ من خذله الله وأهلكه فلا حاجة لكم الى قتله ولعلّه أراد به المعنى الأوّل وخيّل إليهم الثاني لتلين شكيمتهم وعرّض به فرعون بأنّه مسرف كذّاب لا يهديه الله سبيل الصواب. It is said this is a third argument with two aspects (وجهين): The first is that if he were a transgressor (مسرفاً) and a liar (كذّاباً), Allah would not have guided him to the manifest proofs (البيّنات) nor supported him with those miracles (المعجزات). The second is that if Allah has forsaken him and destroyed him, then you have no need to kill him. Perhaps he intended the first meaning and made them imagine the second so that their stubbornness (شكيمتهم) might soften. And he alluded to Pharaoh by this, implying that he is a transgressor (مسرف) and a liar (كذّاب) whom Allah does not guide to the path of righteousness (سبيل الصواب).

(٢٩) يا قَوْمِ لَكُمُ الْمُلْكُ الْيَوْمَ ظاهِرِينَ ** (O my people! Today the sovereignty is yours, being dominant) dominant, superior فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (in the land.) the land of Egypt فَمَنْ يَنْصُرُنا مِنْ بَأْسِ اللهِ إِنْ جاءَنا ** (But who will help us against Allah’s might should it overtake us?) meaning: So do not corrupt your affair and do not expose yourselves to the might of Allah by killing him, for if it overtakes us, no one will protect us from it. And he only included himself in it to show them that he is with them and shares with them in what he advises them. قالَ فِرْعَوْنُ ما أُرِيكُمْ ** (Pharaoh said, 'I do not show you) what I advise you إِلَّا ما أَرى ** (except what I see,) and deem correct regarding his killing وَما أَهْدِيكُمْ إِلَّا سَبِيلَ الرَّشادِ ** (and I do not guide you except to the way of rectitude.') the path of correctness.

(٣٠) وَقالَ الَّذِي آمَنَ يا قَوْمِ إِنِّي أَخافُ عَلَيْكُمْ ** (30) And he who had faith said, 'O my people! Indeed I fear for you regarding their denial of him and their opposition to him, مِثْلَ يَوْمِ الْأَحْزابِ ** the like of the day of the confederates, the like of the days of the past nations who formed confederacies against the Messengers, meaning their calamities. And the plural form al-Ahzab (الْأَحْزابِ), along with the explanation, suffices for the pluralization of al-Yawm (day).

(٣١) مِثْلَ دَأْبِ قَوْمِ نُوحٍ وَعَادٍ وَثَمُودَ ** (31) like the custom of the people of Noah, and 'Ad, and Thamud, like the established way (سنّة) of Allah concerning them, when He uprooted them and destroyed them, as a recompense for what they were upon of disbelief and harming the Messengers. وَالَّذِينَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ (and those who came after them) such as the people of Lot (قوم لوط). وَمَا اللهُ يُرِيدُ ظُلْماً لِلْعِبادِ ** and Allah does not desire any wrong for the servants. So He does not punish them without a sin, nor does He leave the wrongdoer among them without retribution (انتقام).

(٣٢)** وَيا قَوْمِ إِنِّي أَخافُ عَلَيْكُمْ يَوْمَ التَّنادِ ** (32) O my people! Indeed, I fear for you the Day of Calling Out, The Day when some of them call out to others.

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): **يَوْمَ التَّنادِ ** (the Day of Calling Out) is the Day when some of them call out to others.

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): **يَوْمَ التَّنادِ ** (the Day of Calling Out) is the Day when the inhabitants of the Fire call out to the inhabitants of Paradise: أَفِيضُوا عَلَيْنا مِنَ الْماءِ أَوْ مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ اللهُ (Pour down on us some water or some of what Allah has provided you).

(٣٣) يَوْمَ تُوَلُّونَ مُدْبِرِينَ ما لَكُمْ مِنَ اللهِ مِنْ عاصِمٍ ** (33) the day you turn your backs in retreat, having no defender against Allah. (who protects you from His punishment) ** وَمَنْ يُضْلِلِ اللهُ فَما لَهُ مِنْ هادٍ ** And whomever Allah leads astray, there is no guide for him. (٣٤) وَلَقَدْ جاءَكُمْ يُوسُفُ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (34) Certainly Joseph came to you before (Moses) ** بِالْبَيِّناتِ ** with manifest proofs, (with miracles) ** فَما زِلْتُمْ فِي شَكٍّ مِمَّا جاءَكُمْ بِهِ ** but you continued to be skeptical of what he brought you (concerning the religion).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a hadith: He was asked whether Joseph was a Messenger (رسول) and a Prophet (نبي). He said: "Yes. Do you not hear the saying of Allah, the Almighty: لَقَدْ جاءَكُمْ يُوسُفُ مِنْ قَبْلُ بِالْبَيِّناتِ (Certainly Joseph came to you before with manifest proofs)?" And the entirety of this has passed in Surat Yusuf (peace be upon him). ** حَتَّى إِذا هَلَكَ ** until, when he died, (he died) ** قُلْتُمْ لَنْ يَبْعَثَ اللهُ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ رَسُولاً كَذلِكَ يُضِلُّ اللهُ ** you said, ‘Allah will never send any apostle after him.’ That is how Allah leads astray (into disobedience (العصيان)) ** مَنْ هُوَ مُسْرِفٌ مُرْتابٌ ** him who is a transgressor and a skeptic. (doubtful (شاكّ) concerning what the manifest proofs (البيّنات) testify to, due to the dominance of delusion (الوهم) and immersion (الانهماك) in blind imitation (التقليد)).

(٣٥) ** الَّذِينَ يُجادِلُونَ فِي آياتِ اللهِ بِغَيْرِ سُلْطانٍ ** without proof (حجّة) ** أَتاهُمْ ** but rather either by imitation (تقليد) or a refutable (داحضة) doubt (شبهة). ** كَبُرَ مَقْتاً عِنْدَ اللهِ وَعِنْدَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كَذلِكَ يَطْبَعُ اللهُ عَلى كُلِّ قَلْبِ مُتَكَبِّرٍ جَبَّارٍ ** (35) those who dispute the signs of Allah without any authority that He may have brought them. Great is the abhorrence [of this] in the sight of Allah and in the sight of the faithful. Thus does Allah set a seal on every heart of an arrogant tyrant. And قلب was recited with tanwin (بالتّنوين).

(٣٦) وَقالَ فِرْعَوْنُ يا هامانُ ابْنِ لِي صَرْحاً ** an exposed, high structure, derived from (صرّح) the thing when it appeared (إذا ظهر) لَعَلِّي أَبْلُغُ الْأَسْبابَ ** the way (الطريق). (36) Pharaoh said, 'O Haman, build me a tower, that I may reach the ways— (٣٧)* أَسْبابَ السَّماواتِ فَأَطَّلِعَ إِلى إِلهِ مُوسى And it was read (وقرئ) with the nasb (بالنّصب) as a response (جواب) to the hope (الترجي) وَإِنِّي لَأَظُنُّهُ كاذِباً ** regarding the claim of prophethood (في دعوى الرّسالة) وَكَذلِكَ زُيِّنَ لِفِرْعَوْنَ سُوءُ عَمَلِهِ وَصُدَّ عَنِ السَّبِيلِ ** the path of guidance (سبيل الرّشاد) And it was read (وقرئ) (وَصَدَّ) (active voice), meaning that Pharaoh barred (صدّ) the people from guidance (الهدى) through such deceptions (التّمويهات) and ambiguities (الشبهات) وَما كَيْدُ فِرْعَوْنَ إِلَّا فِي تَبابٍ ** meaning ruin (خسار*). (37) the ways of the heavens, so that I may look upon Moses' God, and indeed I think he is a liar.' Thus was Pharaoh's evil deed made to seem good to him, and he was barred from the way. And Pharaoh's stratagem ended only in ruin.

(٣٨) وَقالَ الَّذِي آمَنَ يا قَوْمِ اتَّبِعُونِ أَهْدِكُمْ ** (And he who had faith said, 'O my people! Follow me, I will guide you) by means of indication (بالدلالة) ** سَبِيلَ الرَّشادِ. (to the way of rectitude.) (٣٩) يا قَوْمِ إِنَّما هذِهِ الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا مَتاعٌ ** ('O my people! This life of the world is only a temporary enjoyment,) a slight enjoyment (تمتع يسير) due to the swiftness of its passing (لسرعة زوالها) ** وَإِنَّ الْآخِرَةَ هِيَ دارُ الْقَرارِ ** (and the Hereafter is indeed the abode of permanence.) due to its eternity (لخلودها). (٤٠) مَنْ عَمِلَ سَيِّئَةً فَلا يُجْزى إِلَّا مِثْلَها ** (Whoever commits an evil deed will be requited only with its like,) as justice (عدلاً) from Allah, the Glorified (سبحانه) ** وَمَنْ عَمِلَ صالِحاً مِنْ ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنْثى وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَأُولئِكَ يَدْخُلُونَ الْجَنَّةَ يُرْزَقُونَ فِيها بِغَيْرِ حِسابٍ ** (but whoever does a righteous deed, whether male or female, should he be a believer—such shall enter paradise, and they shall be provided in it without any reckoning.) without estimation (بغير تقدير) or balancing against the deed (وموازنة بالعمل), but rather manifold increases (أضعافاً مضاعفة) as a favor (فضلاً) and mercy (ورحمة) from Allah.

(٤١) وَيا قَوْمِ ما لِي أَدْعُوكُمْ إِلَى النَّجاةِ وَتَدْعُونَنِي إِلَى النَّارِ. (41) And O my people! What is the matter with me that I invite you to salvation, while you invite me to the Fire? (٤٢) تَدْعُونَنِي لِأَكْفُرَ بِاللهِ وَأُشْرِكَ بِهِ ما لَيْسَ لِي بِهِ ** بربوبيّته عِلْمٌ (42) You invite me to disbelieve in Allah and to ascribe to Him partners of which I have no knowledge regarding His Lordship (بربوبيّته)—knowledge (عِلْمٌ). And the intended meaning is the negation of the known (الْمَعْلُوم), and the indication that Divinity (الألوهيّة) must necessarily have a proof (برهان), and belief in it is not valid except through certainty (إيقان). وَأَنَا أَدْعُوكُمْ إِلَى الْعَزِيزِ الْغَفَّارِ ** while I invite you to the All-mighty, the All-forgiving. Who gathers the attributes of Divinity (الألوهيّة), including the perfection of power (القدرة), dominance (الغلبة), the ability to recompense (المجازاة), and the power over punishment and forgiveness (الغفران).

(43) ** لا جَرَمَ ** No denial (لا ردّ) of what they called him to, and jarm (جَرَم) means haqq (حقّ). أَنَّما تَدْعُونَنِي إِلَيْهِ لَيْسَ لَهُ دَعْوَةٌ فِي الدُّنْيا وَلا فِي الْآخِرَةِ ** (43) Certainly, that to which you invite me has no claim to be invoked, either in the world or in the Hereafter, It was said (قيل): Meaning, the certainty (حقّ) of the lack of invitation (عدم دعوة) of your deities to their worship, or the lack of an answered invitation (دعوة مستجابة) for them. وَأَنَّ مَرَدَّنا إِلَى اللهِ ** and that our return is indeed to Allah, By death (بالموت). وَأَنَّ الْمُسْرِفِينَ ** and that the transgressors— In error (الضلالة) and transgression (الطغيان). هُمْ أَصْحابُ النَّارِ.** they are the inmates of the Fire.

(٤٤) فَسَتَذْكُرُونَ مَا أَقُولُ لَكُمْ ** (44) "Soon you will remember what I tell you," upon witnessing the punishment, of advice. And I entrust my affair to Allah so that He may protect me from every evil. ** إِنَّ اللَّهَ بَصِيرٌ بِالْعِبَادِ ** (Indeed Allah is watchful over His servants.) so He guards them. (٤٥) فَوَقَاهُ اللَّهُ سَيِّئَاتِ مَا مَكَرُوا ** (45) "So Allah preserved him from the evils of what they plotted," (meaning) the severities of their plotting. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) says: This means the believer from the family of Pharaoh. ** وَحَاقَ بِآلِ فِرْعَوْنَ سُوءُ الْعَذَابِ. ** (and the evil punishment besieged Pharaoh’s clan.)

In al-Kafi (الكافي) and al-Mahasin (المحاسن), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: Indeed, they assaulted him and killed him, but do you know what He preserved him from? He preserved him from them tempting him away from his religion.

And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrated from him (peace be upon him): By Allah, they cut him into pieces (إِرْباً إِرْباً), but ** فَوَقَاهُ اللَّهُ ** (So Allah preserved him) from them tempting him away from his religion.

And in al-Ihtijaj (الاحتجاج), narrated from him (peace be upon him) in a narration of his, he said: Hizqil (حزقيل) used to invite them to the Oneness (توحيد) of Allah, the prophethood of Musa (peace be upon him), the superiority (تفضيل) of Muhammad over all the Messengers of Allah and His creation, the superiority of Ali ibn Abi Talib and the best of the Imams (peace be upon them) over the rest of the successors (أوصياء) of the Prophets, and to disavowal (البراءة) of the Lordship (ربوبيّة) of Pharaoh. So the informers (الواشون) informed Pharaoh about him and said: "Hizqil (حزقيل) invites people to oppose you and assists your enemies against you." Pharaoh said to them: "He is My cousin, My successor over My kingdom, and My heir apparent (وليّ عهدي). If he has done what you claim, then he deserves punishment for his ingratitude (كفره) toward My favor. But if you are lying against him, then you deserve the severest punishment for preferring to engage in malice (مساءَة)." So he brought Hizqil (حزقيل) and brought them (the informers). They confronted him and said: "Do you deny the Lordship (ربوبيّة) of Pharaoh the King and show ingratitude for his favors?" Hizqil (حزقيل) said: "O King, have you ever experienced a lie from Me?" He said: "No." He said: "Then ask them who their Lord is." They said: "This Pharaoh." He said: "And who is your Creator (خالقكم)?" They said: "This Pharaoh." He said: "And who is your Sustainer (رازقكم), the Guarantor (الكافل) of your livelihoods (معايشكم), and the Repeller (الدافع) of your misfortunes (مكارهكم)?" They said: "This Pharaoh." Hizqil (حزقيل) said: "O King, I call you and everyone present to witness that their Lord is My Lord, their Creator is My Creator, their Sustainer is My Sustainer, and the Reformer (مصلح) of their livelihoods is the Reformer of My livelihood. I have no Lord and no Sustainer other than their Lord, their Creator, and their Sustainer. And I call you and everyone present to witness that every lord, sustainer, and creator besides their Lord, their Creator, and their Sustainer—I disavow him, his Lordship (ربوبيّته), and I reject his divinity (آلهيّته)." Hizqil (حزقيل) said this, meaning that their Lord is Allah, My Lord, but he did not say that the one they claimed was their lord was My Lord. This meaning was hidden from Pharaoh and those present, and they imagined that he was saying: "Pharaoh is My Lord, My Creator, and My Sustainer." Pharaoh said to them: "O men of evil, O seekers of corruption in My kingdom, and O those who desire discord between Me and My cousin, who is My strength (عضُدي)! You are the ones deserving of My punishment because you desired the corruption of My affair, the destruction of My cousin, and the weakening of My strength (عضُدي)." Then he ordered the stakes (الأوتاد). A stake (وتد) was placed in the leg of each one of them and a stake in his chest. Then the men with iron combs (أمشاط الحديد) were brought, and they tore their flesh from their bodies with them. That is what Allah the Almighty said: ** فَوَقَاهُ اللَّهُ سَيِّئَاتِ مَا مَكَرُوا ** (So Allah preserved him from the evils of what they plotted) against him when they informed Pharaoh about him so that he might destroy him. وَحَاقَ بِآلِ فِرْعَوْنَ سُوءُ الْعَذَابِ (and the evil punishment besieged Pharaoh’s clan), and they were those who informed Pharaoh about Hizqil (حزقيل) when he drove the stakes (الأوتاد) into them and combed the flesh from their bodies with the combs (الأمشاط).

(٤٦) النَّارُ يُعْرَضُونَ عَلَيْها غُدُوًّا وَعَشِيًّا. (46) The Fire, they are exposed to it morning and evening.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That is in the world (الدنيا) before the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), because in the Fire of Resurrection there will be no morning (غدوّ) and evening (عشيّ). Then He said: If they were only being tormented in the Fire morning and evening, then in between that they would be among the fortunate (السعداء). No, rather this is in the Fire of the Intermediate Realm (نار البرزخ) before the Day of Resurrection. Have you not heard His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: **وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ ** (the Ayah)?

And al-Qummi said: He meant that this is in the world (الدنيا) before the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), and that is because on the Day of Resurrection there will be no morning (غدوّ) nor evening (عشاء), for the morning and evening only occur due to the sun and the moon, and there is neither sun nor moon in the Gardens of Eternity (جنان الخلد) nor in their Fires (نيرانها).

He said: And al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "What do the people say concerning it?" It was said: "They say that it is in the Fire of Eternity (نار الخلد), and that they are not tormented in between that." He (peace be upon him) said: "Then they are among the fortunate (السعداء)." Then He said: "This is only in the world (الدنيا). As for the Fire of Eternity (نار الخلد), it is His saying: **وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ ** (the Ayah)."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): The souls (أرواح) of the disbelievers are in the Fire of Hell (نار جهنّم), they are exposed to it (يُعْرَضُونَ عَلَيْها). They say: "Our Lord, do not establish the Hour (الساعة) for us, and do not fulfill for us what You promised us, and do not join our last ones to our first ones."

And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah the Almighty has a Fire in the East which He created for the souls (أرواح) of the disbelievers to dwell in. They eat from its zaqqum (زقّومها) and drink from its boiling water (حميمها) during their night. When the dawn breaks, it stirs up towards a valley in Yemen called Barhut (برهوت), which is hotter than the fire of the world (نار الدنيا). They meet and recognize one another there. When the evening comes, they return to the Fire. They remain thus until the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة).

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: He said: "Indeed, when one of you dies, his seat is displayed to him in the morning (بالغداة) and the evening (والعشيّ). If he is among the people of Paradise (أهل الجنّة), then it is from Paradise; and if he is among the people of the Fire (أهل النار), then it is from the Fire. It is said: 'This is your seat until Allah resurrects you on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة).'"

**وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ أَدْخِلُوا آلَ فِرْعَوْنَ أَشَدَّ الْعَذابِ ** (And the day the Hour is established: 'Admit the clan of Pharaoh into the severest punishment!')

And it was read (ادخلوا) with two dammahs.

(٤٧) وَإِذْ يَتَحاجُّونَ فِي النَّارِ فَيَقُولُ الضُّعَفاءُ لِلَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا إِنَّا كُنَّا لَكُمْ تَبَعاً فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُغْنُونَ عَنَّا نَصِيباً مِنَ النَّارِ ** (47) And when they contend with one another in the Fire, the weak will say to those who were arrogant, 'Indeed we were your followers. Will you then relieve us of a share of the Fire?' (This relief is) by warding it off (بالدفع) or bearing it (الحمل).

In Misbah al-Mutahajjid, in a sermon by the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), which He delivered on the Day of Ghadir, and in which He recited this Ayah, He then said: Do you know what arrogance (الاستكبار) is? It is abandoning obedience (ترك الطّاعة) to those whose obedience they were commanded, and elevating oneself (والترفّع) above those whom they were urged to follow. And the Quran speaks much concerning this.

(٤٨) قالَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا إِنَّا كُلٌّ فِيها ** (48) Those who were arrogant will say, 'All of us are in it.' (Meaning:) We and you (are in it), so how can we avail you? If we had the power, we would have availed ourselves. ** إِنَّ اللهَ قَدْ حَكَمَ بَيْنَ الْعِبادِ ** 'Indeed Allah has judged between the servants.' And there is no one to overturn His judgment. (٤٩) وَقالَ الَّذِينَ فِي النَّارِ لِخَزَنَةِ جَهَنَّمَ ادْعُوا رَبَّكُمْ يُخَفِّفْ عَنَّا يَوْماً مِنَ الْعَذابِ. (49) Those who are in the Fire will say to the keepers of hell, 'Invoke your Lord to lighten the punishment for us for a day.' (٥٠) قالُوا أَوَلَمْ تَكُ تَأْتِيكُمْ رُسُلُكُمْ بِالْبَيِّناتِ ** (50) They will say, 'Did not your apostles bring you manifest proofs?' They intended by this to establish the proof (الحجّة) against them and to rebuke them for wasting the times of supplication (الدعاء) and neglecting the means of response (أسباب الإجابة). ** قالُوا بَلى قالُوا فَادْعُوا ** They will say, 'Yes, they did.' They will say, 'Then invoke [Him].' (Meaning:) For we do not dare to do so, as we have not been permitted to supplicate (الدّعاء) for the likes of you. And in this is a cutting off of their hope (اقناط) for a response. ** وَما دُعاءُ الْكافِرِينَ إِلَّا فِي ضَلالٍ ** 'The invocation of the faithless is only in futility.' (Meaning:) In loss (ضياع), which is not answered.

(٥١) إِنَّا لَنَنْصُرُ رُسُلَنا وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا وَيَوْمَ يَقُومُ الْأَشْهادُ. (51) Indeed We will surely help Our apostles and those who have faith, in the life of the world and on the day when the witnesses rise up. Al-Qummi said: It means the Imams (peace be upon them). And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That, by Allah, is concerning the Return (الرجعة). Did you not know that many prophets were not helped in this world and were killed, and the Imams (peace be upon them) after them were killed and were not helped? That is concerning the Return (الرجعة).

(٥٢) يَوْمَ لا يَنْفَعُ الظَّالِمِينَ مَعْذِرَتُهُمْ ** (52) The day when the wrongdoers’ excuses will not benefit them, due to their invalidity (لبطلانها). And it was read (وقرئ) with the tā’ (بالتاء) [as تَنْفَعُ]. وَلَهُمُ اللَّعْنَةُ ** (and for them is the curse) which is the distance from mercy (البعد من الرحمة). وَلَهُمْ سُوءُ الدَّارِ ** (and for them is the evil abode) which is Hell (جهنّم).

(٥٣) وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا مُوسَى الْهُدى ** (53) Certainly We gave Moses the guidance, that by which guidance is attained in religion (ما يهتدي به في الدّين), consisting of miracles (المعجزات), scriptures (الصحف), and laws (الشرايع). وَأَوْرَثْنا بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ الْكِتابَ ** (and made the Children of Israel heirs to the Book) and We left upon them after him, from that [guidance], the Torah (التوراة). هُدىً وَذِكْرى (as a guidance and a reminder) meaning guidance (هداية) and a reminder (تذكرة) لِأُولِي الْأَلْبابِ (for those who possess intellect) meaning for those possessing sound minds (لذوي العقول السّليمة).

(55) ** فَاصْبِرْ ** (So be patient) concerning the harm (أذى) of the polytheists إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللهِ حَقٌ ** (Allah’s promise is indeed true) with victory. And plead for forgiveness for your sin (ذنب), which is for leaving what was better (الأولى) and being concerned with the affair of the enemies (العدى). وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ بِالْعَشِيِّ وَالْإِبْكارِ.** (55) So be patient; Allah’s promise is indeed true. Plead for forgiveness for your sin, and celebrate the praise of your Lord evening and morning.

(56) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُجادِلُونَ فِي آياتِ اللهِ بِغَيْرِ سُلْطانٍ أَتاهُمْ ** (Indeed those who dispute concerning the signs of Allah without any authority that may have come to them) is general (عامّ) regarding every disputer (مجادل) who is false (مبطل), even if it was revealed concerning the polytheists of Mecca or the Jews, according to what has been said. إِنْ فِي صُدُورِهِمْ إِلَّا كِبْرٌ ** (there is nothing in their breasts but pride)—except greatness (عظمة) and arrogance (تكبّر) against the truth (الحقّ). ما هُمْ بِبالِغِيهِ ** (which they will not satisfy/reach)—they will not reach the requirement (مقتضى) of that greatness (العظمة) because Allah will humiliate them (مذلّهم). So فَاسْتَعِذْ بِاللهِ ** (seek refuge in Allah)—so resort to Him. **إِنَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْبَصِيرُ ** (indeed He is the All-hearing, the All-seeing) of your sayings and your deeds. (56) Indeed those who dispute concerning the signs of Allah without any authority that may have come to them—there is nothing in their breasts but pride, which they will not satisfy. So seek refuge in Allah; indeed He is the All-hearing, the All-seeing.

(٥٧) ** لَخَلْقُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ أَكْبَرُ مِنْ خَلْقِ النَّاسِ ** (57) The creation of the heavens and the earth is surely greater than the creation of mankind,

So whoever was able to create them first without an origin (أصل), is able to create mankind secondly from an origin (أصل) in this manner. It was said: وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يَعْلَمُونَ ** (but most people do not know) because they do not look nor contemplate due to the excess of their heedlessness (غفلتهم) and their following of their desires (أهوائهم).

(٥٨) ** وَما يَسْتَوِي الْأَعْمى وَالْبَصِيرُ ** (The blind and the seeing are not equal) The ignorant (الجاهل) and the insightful (المستبصر). ** وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ وَلَا الْمُسِيءُ ** (nor those who have faith and do righteous deeds and the evildoer) And the good-doer (المحسن) and the evildoer (المسيء). So it is necessary that there be a state for them in which the difference becomes manifest, and that is what is after the Resurrection (البعث). ** قَلِيلاً ما تَتَذَكَّرُونَ ** (Seldom do you take admonition!) And it was read with the tā' (التاء).

(٥٩) إِنَّ السَّاعَةَ لَآتِيَةٌ لا رَيْبَ فِيها ** (The Hour is indeed coming; there is no doubt in it.) regarding its arrival وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ ** (But most people do not believe.) they do not affirm it, due to the limitation of their sight to the apparent things they perceive. (٦٠) وَقالَ رَبُّكُمُ ادْعُونِي أَسْتَجِبْ لَكُمْ إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ عَنْ عِبادَتِي ** (Your Lord has said, 'Invoke Me and I will respond to you. Indeed, those who are too arrogant to worship Me) meaning: My supplication (دعائي) سَيَدْخُلُونَ جَهَنَّمَ داخِرِينَ (will soon enter hell abased) meaning: humiliated (صاغرين). And it was recited (وقرئ) سَيُدْخَلُونَ (sayudkhalūna) with a ḍammah on the yā (بضمّ الياء) and a fatḥah on the khā (وفتح الخاء).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: "It is supplication (الدعاء), and the best form of worship (العبادة) is supplication (الدعاء)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked which form of worship (العبادة) is best, so he said to him: "There is nothing better in the sight of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), than that He be asked (يسئل) and that what is with Him be sought (يطلب ما عنده). And there is no one more hateful to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), than one who is too arrogant to worship Him (يستكبر عن عبادته) and does not ask for what is with Him."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Supplicate (ادع) and do not say, 'The matter has already been settled (قد فرغ من الأمر),' for indeed, supplication (الدعاء) is worship (العبادة)." Indeed, Allah says [and he recited this verse].

In al-Sahifah al-Sajjadiyyah, after mentioning this verse: "So You named Your supplication (دُعاءَكَ) worship (عِبادَةً), and the abandonment of it arrogance (اسْتِكْباراً), and You threatened entry into Hell, abased (دُخُولَ جَهَنَّمَ داخِرِينَ), for abandoning it."

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked: "Does not Allah say: ادْعُونِي أَسْتَجِبْ لَكُمْ (Invoke Me and I will respond to you)? Yet we see the distressed person (المضطرّ) invoking Him and He is not answered, and the oppressed person (المظلوم) seeking His help against his enemy and He does not grant him victory." He said: "Woe unto you! No one invokes Him except that He responds to him. As for the wrongdoer (الظالم), his supplication is rejected until he repents. As for the rightful person (المحقّ), when he invokes Him, He responds to him and averts the affliction (البلاء) from him in a way he does not know, or He stores up for him a great reward (ثواباً جزيلاً) for the day he is in need of it. And if the matter which the servant asked for was not good for him if He granted it to him, He withholds it from him. And the believer (المؤمن), the one who knows Allah (العارف بالله), sometimes finds it difficult (عزّ عليه) to invoke Him regarding something when he does not know whether that is correct (صواب) or wrong (خطأ)." And other narratives (أخبار أخر) concerning this meaning have already passed in Surat al-Baqarah regarding the Almighty's saying: أُجِيبُ دَعْوَةَ الدَّاعِ إِذا دَعانِ. (I answer the supplicant’s call when he calls Me.)

(٦١) اللهُ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ اللَّيْلَ لِتَسْكُنُوا فِيهِ ** (61) Allah is He who made the night for you, that you may rest in it, that you may rest (لتستريحوا) in it, by creating it cold and dark, so that it leads to the weakening of the movers (المحركات) or the calming of the senses (الحواس). وَالنَّهارَ مُبْصِراً ** and the day sight-giving. meaning one sees in it or by means of it. Attributing sight (الإبصار) to it (the day) is a metaphor (مجاز) containing hyperbole (مبالغة). إِنَّ اللهَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى النَّاسِ ** Indeed Allah is gracious to mankind, a grace (فضل) that no grace can equal. وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يَشْكُرُونَ ** but most people do not give thanks. due to their ignorance of the Bestower of blessings (المنعم) and their heedlessness concerning the places where the blessings descend (مواقع النعم).

(٦٢) ذلِكُمُ اللهُ رَبُّكُمْ خالِقُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ فَأَنَّى تُؤْفَكُونَ ** (62) That is Allah, your Lord, the Creator of all things. There is no god except Him. So where are you being led away? (تُؤْفَكُونَ) means: you are being turned away from His worship to the worship of other than Him. (٦٣) كَذلِكَ يُؤْفَكُ الَّذِينَ كانُوا بِآياتِ اللهِ يَجْحَدُونَ. (63) Thus are turned away those who used to impugn the signs of Allah. (٦٤) اللهُ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَرْضَ قَراراً وَالسَّماءَ بِناءً وَصَوَّرَكُمْ فَأَحْسَنَ صُوَرَكُمْ ** (64) It is Allah who made the earth a resting place for you, and the sky a canopy, and He formed you, and perfected your forms, (فَأَحْسَنَ صُوَرَكُمْ) means: by creating you upright in stature, manifest in skin, proportionate in limbs and contours, and prepared for practicing crafts and acquiring perfections. ** وَرَزَقَكُمْ مِنَ الطَّيِّباتِ ** the delights (اللّذائذ) ** ذلِكُمُ اللهُ رَبُّكُمْ فَتَبارَكَ اللهُ رَبُّ الْعالَمِينَ ** and provided you with good things. That is Allah, your Lord. So blessed is Allah, the Lord of all the worlds. for everything other than Him is nurtured (مربوب), inherently needy (مفتقر بالذات), and subject to perishing (معرض للزوال).

(٦٥) ** هُوَ الْحَيُ ** (65) He is the Living [One]. The One Who is unique in possessing essential (الذّاتية) life. لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ** (There is no god except Him.) No one equals Him or approaches Him in His Essence (ذاته) or His attributes (صفاته). فَادْعُوهُ ** (So supplicate Him,) So worship Him. مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ ** (dedicating your worship to Him.) [Purifying it] from polytheism (الشرك) and hypocrisy (الرياء). الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.) Saying to Him. Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): When one of you says لا اله إلّا الله (There is no god except Allah), he should then say الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ (All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds), for Allah says: هُوَ الْحَيُ ** (He is the Living [One])—the Ayah.

(٦٦) قُلْ إِنِّي نُهِيتُ أَنْ أَعْبُدَ الَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ لَمَّا جاءَنِي الْبَيِّناتُ مِنْ رَبِّي وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أُسْلِمَ لِرَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (66) Say, 'I have been forbidden to worship those whom you invoke besides Allah, since manifest proofs have come to me from my Lord, and I have been commanded to submit to the Lord of all the worlds.' That is, that I submit (أن أنقاد) and make my religion sincere (أخلص له ديني) for Him.

(٦٧) هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ تُرابٍ ثُمَّ مِنْ نُطْفَةٍ ثُمَّ مِنْ عَلَقَةٍ ثُمَّ يُخْرِجُكُمْ طِفْلاً ثُمَّ لِتَبْلُغُوا ** Then He keeps you alive أَشُدَّكُمْ ثُمَّ لِتَكُونُوا شُيُوخاً وَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يُتَوَفَّى مِنْ قَبْلُ ** Before old age (الشيخوخة) or reaching maturity (بلوغ الأشدّ). وَلِتَبْلُغُوا (And that you may reach) — and He does that لِتَبْلُغُوا أَجَلاً مُسَمًّى (so that you may reach a specified term) — the time of death وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ ** (67) It is He who created you from dust, then from a drop of semen, then from a clinging mass, then He brings you forth as infants, then [He sustains you] so that you may reach your maturity, then that you may become aged—though there are some of you who are taken away earlier—and so that you may reach a specified term, and so that you may apply reason. What is in that of proofs (الحجج) and lessons (العبر).

(٦٨) هُوَ الَّذِي يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ فَإِذا قَضى أَمْراً ** (68) He is the One who gives life and brings death. When He decrees a matter When He wills it, ** فَإِنَّما يَقُولُ لَهُ كُنْ فَيَكُونُ ** He only says to it, ‘Be,’ and it is. without preparation, or undertaking difficulty, without sound and without letter. And the first fāʾ (الفاء) is to indicate that that is a result of what preceded.

(٦٩) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِينَ يُجادِلُونَ فِي آياتِ اللهِ أَنَّى يُصْرَفُونَ ** (69) Have you not regarded those who dispute concerning the signs of Allah, how they are turned away? from believing in them. (٧٠) الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِالْكِتابِ وَبِما أَرْسَلْنا بِهِ رُسُلَنا فَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ ** (70) Those who denied the Book and that with which We sent Our apostles—soon they will know, the recompense for their denial. (٧١) إِذِ الْأَغْلالُ فِي أَعْناقِهِمْ وَالسَّلاسِلُ يُسْحَبُونَ ** (71) when the fetters are around their necks, and the chains, as they are dragged by them. (٧٢) فِي الْحَمِيمِ ثُمَّ فِي النَّارِ يُسْجَرُونَ ** (72) into the boiling water, then they are burned in the Fire. they are burned. (٧٣) ثُمَّ قِيلَ لَهُمْ أَيْنَ ما كُنْتُمْ تُشْرِكُونَ. ** (73) Then they will be told, ‘Where are those whom you used to take as partners (٧٤) مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ قالُوا ضَلُّوا عَنَّا ** (74) besides Allah?’ They will say, ‘They have failed us. They have been lost to us, and we did not find what we used to expect from them. ** بَلْ لَمْ نَكُنْ نَدْعُوا مِنْ قَبْلُ شَيْئاً ** Rather, we did not call upon anything before.’ Rather, it has become clear to us that we were not worshipping anything through our worship of them.

In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "As for the Nussab (النصاب) among the people of the Qiblah (أهل القبلة), a trench (خدّاً) will be dug for them toward the Fire which Allah created in the East. From it, the flames, sparks, smoke, and the surge of boiling water (فورة الحميم) will enter upon them until the Day of Resurrection. Then their destination will be the boiling water (الحميم): ** ثُمَّ فِي النَّارِ يُسْجَرُونَ ثُمَّ قِيلَ لَهُمْ أَيْنَ ما كُنْتُمْ تُشْرِكُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** (then they are burned in the Fire. Then they will be told, ‘Where are those whom you used to take as partners besides Allah?’) meaning: Where is your Imam whom you took instead of the Imam Whom Allah appointed as an Imam for the people?"

And in al-Basa'ir, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "I was behind my father (Imam al-Sajjad) while He was upon His mule. His mule shied away, and behold, there was an old man with a chain around his neck, and a man following him. He said: 'O Ali ibn al-Husayn, give me water to drink.' The man said: 'Do not give him water to drink; may Allah not give him water to drink.' The old man was Mu'awiyah." And there are other narrations with this meaning. ** كَذلِكَ يُضِلُّ اللهُ الْكافِرِينَ ** (Thus does Allah lead the faithless astray) so that they are not guided to anything that benefits them in the Hereafter.

Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, said: "Allah has indeed named them disbelievers (كافرين) and polytheists (مشركين) because they denied the Book. Allah sent His messengers with the Book and with its interpretation (تأويله). So, whoever denies the Book, or denies that which Allah sent His messengers with concerning the interpretation of the Book, he is a polytheist (مشرك) and a disbeliever (كافر)."

(٧٥) ذلِكُمْ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَفْرَحُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (75) That is because of what you used to rejoice in the land You were insolent and arrogant. بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِ Which is polytheism (الشرك) and transgression (الطغيان). وَبِما كُنْتُمْ تَمْرَحُونَ You were excessive in joy. (٧٦) ادْخُلُوا أَبْوابَ جَهَنَّمَ ** (76) Enter the gates of hell The seven gates designated for you. خالِدِينَ فِيها Predestined for eternity. فَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى الْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ Hell is the wretched abode of those who are arrogant against the truth (الحقّ). (٧٧) فَاصْبِرْ إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللهِ ** (77) So be patient; Allah’s promise Regarding the destruction of the disbelievers (الكفّار) and their punishment, حَقٌ is inevitable. فَإِمَّا نُرِيَنَّكَ Meaning: If We show you (فان نُرِكَ). The mā (ما) is extra (مزيدة) for emphasizing the conditional nature (الشّرطيّة), and for this reason, the nūn (النون) is affixed to the verb (الفعل). بَعْضَ الَّذِي نَعِدُهُمْ Which is the killing (القتل) and the captivity (الأسر). أَوْ نَتَوَفَّيَنَّكَ Before you see it. فَإِلَيْنا يُرْجَعُونَ **On the Day of Resurrection, and We shall recompense them for their deeds.

(٧٨) وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا رُسُلاً مِنْ قَبْلِكَ مِنْهُمْ مَنْ قَصَصْنا عَلَيْكَ وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ لَمْ نَقْصُصْ عَلَيْكَ. (78) Certainly We have sent apostles before you. Of them are those We have recounted to you, and of them are those We have not recounted to you.

In al-Khisal, narrated from them (peace be upon them): Their number is one hundred and twenty-four thousand.

And in al-Majma', narrated from Ali: Allah sent a black prophet whose story He did not recount to us.

**وَما كانَ لِرَسُولٍ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ بِآيَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللهِ ** And it is not for any apostle to bring a sign except by Allah’s leave.

For the miracles (المعجزات) are gifts which He distributed among them according to what His wisdom necessitated. They have no choice in preferring some of them or acting independently in bringing forth what is proposed/requested of them. So when the command of Allah comes with the punishment in this world and the Hereafter, قُضِيَ بِالْحَقِ ** (judgment will be made with justice) by saving the righteous (المحقّ) and punishing the false (المبطل). وَخَسِرَ هُنالِكَ الْمُبْطِلُونَ ** (and it is there that the falsifiers will be losers)—the obstinate ones (المعاندون) who proposed signs after the manifestation of that which suffices them instead of those signs.

(٧٩) اللهُ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَنْعامَ لِتَرْكَبُوا مِنْها وَمِنْها تَأْكُلُونَ ** (79) Allah is He who made the cattle for you, that you may ride some of them, and some of them you may eat. For some of them are eaten, like sheep (الغنم), and some of them are eaten and ridden, like camels (الإبل) and cattle (البقر). (٨٠) وَلَكُمْ فِيها مَنافِعُ ** (80) And there are [other] benefits for you in them, like milk (الألبان), hides (الجلود), and wool/fleece (الأوبار). وَلِتَبْلُغُوا عَلَيْها حاجَةً فِي صُدُورِكُمْ ** and that you may attain through them a need in your breasts, by traveling upon them. وَعَلَيْها ** and upon them, on land (البرّ), وَعَلَى الْفُلْكِ ** and upon the ships, on the sea (البحر), تُحْمَلُونَ.** you are carried.

(٨١) وَيُرِيكُمْ آياتِهِ ** (And He shows you His signs) (which indicate the perfection of His power and the abundance of His mercy) فَأَيَّ آياتِ اللهِ تُنْكِرُونَ ** (So which of Allah’s signs do you deny?) for due to their clarity, they do not accept denial. (٨٢) أَفَلَمْ يَسِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَيَنْظُرُوا كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ كانُوا أَكْثَرَ مِنْهُمْ وَأَشَدَّ than them قُوَّةً وَآثاراً فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (Have they not travelled in the land so that they may observe how was the fate of those who were before them? They were more numerous than them and greater in power and in monuments in the land.) meaning what remained of them, such as palaces (القصور), structures (المصانع), and other things. فَما أَغْنى عَنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ ** (Yet what they used to earn did not avail them.) The first ما (mā) potentially carries the meaning of negation (النافية) or interrogation (الاستفهاميّة), and the second ما (mā) potentially carries the meaning of the relative pronoun (الموصولة) or the verbal noun marker (المصدريّة).

(٨٣) فَلَمَّا جاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ بِالْبَيِّناتِ فَرِحُوا بِما عِنْدَهُمْ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ ** (83) When their apostles brought them manifest proofs, they exulted in the knowledge they had, واستحقروا علم الرّسل وَحاقَ بِهِمْ ما كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ.** and they belittled the knowledge of the Messengers. (83) and what they used to ridicule besieged them.

(٨٤) فَلَمَّا رَأَوْا بَأْسَنا ** (84) So when they saw Our might, شدّة عذابنا قالُوا آمَنَّا بِاللهِ وَحْدَهُ وَكَفَرْنا بِما كُنَّا بِهِ مُشْرِكِينَ ** (meaning) the severity of Our punishment. (84) they said, 'We believe in Allah alone, and we disavow what we used to associate with Him.' يعنون الأصنام. They mean the idols (الأصنام).

(٨٥) فَلَمْ يَكُ يَنْفَعُهُمْ إِيمانُهُمْ لَمَّا رَأَوْا بَأْسَنا ** (85) But their faith was of no avail to them when they saw Our might— لأنّه غير مقبول حينئذ سُنَّتَ اللهِ الَّتِي قَدْ خَلَتْ فِي عِبادِهِ because it is not accepted at that time. سنّ الله ذلك سنّة ماضية في العباد وَخَسِرَ هُنالِكَ الْكافِرُونَ ** Allah established that as an enduring practice (سُنَّة) among the servants. (85) the سنت of Allah which has been operative for His servants— and the faithless were ruined there. أي وقت رؤيتهم البأس استعير اسم المكان للزمان. meaning, at the time of their seeing the might (البأس). The name of the place (اسم المكان) is used metaphorically for the time (الزمان).

في العيون عن الرضا عليه السلام : أنّه سئل لأيّ علّة غرق الله تعالى فرعون وقد آمن به وأقرّ بتوحيده قال لأنّه آمن عند رؤية البأس والايمان عند رؤية البأس غير مقبول وذلك حكم الله تعالى ذكره في السّلف والخلف قال الله عزّ وجلّ فَلَمَّا رَأَوْا بَأْسَنا الآيتين. In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked for what reason Allah the Almighty drowned Pharaoh, even though he had believed in Him and affirmed His Oneness (توحيده). He said: "Because he believed when he saw the might (البأس), and faith when seeing the might (البأس) is not accepted. That is the ruling (حكم) of Allah, exalted be His remembrance, concerning the predecessors (السّلف) and the successors (الخلف)." Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: ** فَلَمَّا رَأَوْا بَأْسَنا** (So when they saw Our might)— referring to the two verses.

وفي الكافي : قدم الى المتوكّل رجل نصرانيّ فجر بامرأة مسلمة فأراد ان يقيم عليه الحدّ فأسلم فقيل قد هدم إيمانه شركه وفعله وقيل يضرب ثلاثة حدود وقيل غير ذلك فأرسل المتوكّل الى الهادي عليه السلام وسأله عن ذلك فكتب عليه السلام يضرب حتّى يموت فأنكروا ذلك وقالوا هذا شيء لم ينطق به كتاب ولم تجيء به سنّة فسلوه ثانياً البيان فكتب هاتين الآيتين بعد البسملة فأمر به المتوكّل فضرب حتّى مات. And in al-Kafi: A Christian man was brought before al-Mutawakkil who had committed adultery with a Muslim woman. [Al-Mutawakkil] intended to carry out the prescribed punishment (الحدّ) upon him, but the man embraced Islam. It was said: "His faith has nullified his polytheism (شركه) and his deed." And it was said: "He should be given three prescribed punishments (حدود)." And it was said: "Something other than that." So al-Mutawakkil sent [a messenger] to al-Hadi (peace be upon him) and asked Him about that. He (peace be upon him) wrote: "He is to be struck until he dies." They rejected this and said: "This is something that no Book has spoken of, nor has any Sunnah brought it. Ask Him a second time for the clarification (البيان)." So He (peace be upon him) wrote these two verses after the Basmalah. Al-Mutawakkil then commanded that he be struck until he died.

في ثواب الأعمال والمجمع عن الباقر عليه السلام قال : من قرأ حم المؤمن في كلّ ليلة غفر الله له ما تقدّم من ذنبه وما تأخّر والزمه كلمة التقوى وجعل الآخرة خيراً له من الدنيا. In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "Whoever recites Ha Mim al-Mu'min (حم المؤمن) every night, Allah will forgive him what has preceded of his sin and what has been delayed, and He will make the word of piety (كلمة التقوى) binding upon him, and He will make the Hereafter better for him than the world."

وعن الصادق عليه السلام : الحواميم رياحين القرآن. And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "The Hawamim (الحواميم) are the fragrant herbs (رياحين) of the Quran."
